expression.description: Arithmetic expression using numbers and supported operators.

function.name: current_time
function.description: Get the current local, UTC, or requested timezone time.

timezone.description: Optional timezone name such as local, UTC, Asia/Shanghai, or a fixed offset like +08:00.

function.name: file_reader
function.description: Read a local text-like file or Office Open XML document from allowed workspace roots. Supports txt, md, json, jsonl, csv, tsv, yaml, yml, py, log, docx, and pptx.

path.description: Path relative to the configured data root.

max_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to return.

start_line.description: Optional 1-based start line for partial reading.

end_line.description: Optional 1-based end line for partial reading.

source.description: Normalized path relative to data root.

slide_count.description: Number of slides for pptx files, when applicable.

function.name: file_writer
function.description: Write a new real file into this run's generated_files output directory. Use this when the user asks to create, generate, or write a file/document with a concrete type or suffix, including txt/.txt, md/.md/markdown, docx/.docx/Word, or supported code files such as .py, .js, .ts, .tsx, .html, .css, .java, .cpp, .c, .h, .cs, .go, .rs, .sql, .sh, or .ps1. Language-named code file requests such as Python, JavaScript, C, C++, Java, Go, Rust, SQL, Shell, or PowerShell code files also use this tool. Never use it to modify existing project files, and do not answer with only inline file content when a real file is requested.

filename.description: Relative output filename with a supported suffix, such as report.md or hello_world.c. Absolute paths and parent-directory segments are rejected.

file_type.description: One of txt, markdown, docx, or code. The filename suffix must match the selected file_type.

generated_file_path.description: Absolute path of the generated file.

relative_output_path.description: Path relative to the run output directory, under generated_files/.

filename.description: Final generated filename after uniqueness handling.

num_bytes.description: Size of the generated file in bytes.

overwritten.description: Always false because file_writer never overwrites existing files.

function.name: mcp_web_search
function.description: Search the web through a configured MCP search server. Use this for internet search, live news, recent events, current external information, or topics not contained in local files.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results requested from the MCP search tool.

mcp_config.description: MCP configuration file used by the bridge.

mcp_arguments.description: Arguments sent to the successful or final MCP tool attempt.

mcp_attempt_count.description: Number of MCP search attempts performed.

mcp_attempts.description: MCP search attempts tried, including fallback attempts and error text.

structured_content.description: Structured content returned by the MCP server when available.

text.description: Text content returned by the MCP server.

is_error.description: Whether the MCP tool reported an error.

function.name: local_file_search
function.description: Search local text-like files under allowed workspace roots for query terms. This is only for local project files; do not use it for web, internet, live news, or current external information.

function.name: table_analyzer
function.description: Analyze a local CSV or TSV file.

path.description: Table path relative to data root.

function.name: format_converter
function.description: Convert text to markdown bullets or formatted JSON and write the converted content to a file.

output_filename.description: Optional output filename. Defaults to converted.md or converted.json.

generated_file_path.description: Path of the generated output file.
